what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view B2 in area B, titled 'Voice / listening mode'. It records which journeys (J4, J7) and cross-journeys (J7) the surface belongs to, lists the ui:// addresses it is built against, and points to the components, design lint command, and token source used to build it.
observations: Surface id is 'B2', area 'B', title 'Voice / listening mode', status 'planned'.
Represents the 'VOICE' modality and targets both desktop and mobile platforms.
Home journey is J4; journeys list includes J4 and J7; cross_journeys lists J7.
Addresses cover chat (debrief, mic, record), settings (voice), and walkthrough (voice).
build_to names components chat, settings, and walkthrough; a design_lint command; and a tokens mapping from design/_system/tokens.json to design-system.css.
The file notes it was generated by a Claude Code session and instructs not to invent addresses.
standouts: A planned voice/listening surface with cross-journey linkage to J7.
Concrete ui:// addresses spanning three components (chat, settings, walkthrough).
build_to combines component targets, a lint gate, and a token-source mapping.
conventions: Follows a JSON surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses a journey-spine directory path (surfaces/J4/B2.json) aligned with home_journey.
Addresses use a ui:// scheme and are declared as non-invented.
Includes an empty symbols array to mark that no code symbols are present.
concerns: Status is 'planned' but the file already contains specific build commands and addresses.
The design_lint command references an external path (canvas/app/src) that cannot be verified from this file.
The distinction between journeys and cross_journeys is not defined here.
symbols is empty despite the file describing build-to components.
notes: This is a planned voice/listening surface spec that ties view B2 to journeys J4/J7, five ui:// addresses, and build targets across chat, settings, and walkthrough.
questions: What is the exact semantics of cross_journeys versus journeys?
Where do the ui:// addresses resolve in code?
What does the FORM gate in the lint command enforce?
How is B3 related to the 'web' mentioned in the _what field?
Which component owns each listed address?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view B2 with journey/address/build mappings, and the file content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Generated design artifacts with provenance constraints.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a traceable design-to-code contract for a voice/listening UI surface, anchoring it to specific journeys, concrete UI addresses, and the downstream build, lint, and token artifacts needed to implement it.
novelty: The explicit 'never invent an address' provenance rule and the inclusion of both a lint gate and a token-source mapping inside a surface spec are relatively distinct.
contribution: It contributes a planned voice/listening surface definition that links a view identifier (B2) to journey references (J4, J7), concrete UI addresses, and build targets/components, providing a traceable anchor between design intent and implementation artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/blueprint/surfaces/J4/B2.json is a planned generated surface specification for view B2 ("Voice / listening mode", VOICE modality) that maps journeys J4 and J7, cross-journey J7, five ui:// addresses across chat, settings, and walkthrough, and build targets including components, a design lint command, and a design token source.
intention_for_embedding: This file intends to specify the design surface and implementation anchors for a voice/listening mode view so it can be traced to journeys, UI addresses, components, lint gates, and design tokens.
